entry.title: SAP Business Network: Supporting suppliers through the new UI experience (SBN on BTP)
entry.content:
As SAP Business Network suppliers and buyers begin to experience the new UI—often referred to as NextGen or SAP Business Network on SAP BTP—we’re hearing a clear ask: What’s changing, what’s not, and how do I prepare?

Our goal is simple: help suppliers' awareness and readiness and help buyers support their suppliers through this transition.

To respond quickly and effectively, we started a dedicated webinar series to walk through the new supplier experience, via a live demo, and answer real supplier questions live.

The first session already took place, presented by @BenjiT.  If you couldn’t attend, here’s a quick snapshot of the key takeaways to get you started:

What is not changing?

Suppliers can rest assured that this transition does not impact the foundation of their account:

  • Account type (Standard, Enterprise, Free)
  • Pricing model
  • Existing customer relationships
  • User access, documents, and historical data
  • Supplier configuration.

In short, these remain intact.

What is changing?

This shift is primarily a navigation and user experience change.

Suppliers are gradually moving to a new interface designed to improve supplier experience, usability, adoption and buyer-supplier collaboration on the Network. While the concepts remain familiar, many menus and features are presented differently.

The rollout for existing supplier accounts happens gradually between April and December.  Whilst exact dates cannot be shared in advance, suppliers will receive an official notification before their account transitions.  New accounts already started being created via Walk-up registration since December 2025 . 

What should suppliers do next?

Whether you are a supplier preparing for the change—or a buyer helping suppliers through it—these two steps are the best place to start:

  1. Visit the SAP Business Network for Suppliers Community page
    For the latest updates, guidance, and resources related to the new UI.
    👉Check the top section for the newest information.
  2. Watch the 30‑minute webinar replay
    👉 Get a guided tour of the new supplier experience, hear live questions answered, and review the supporting materials shared during the session.

Watch out for future webinar sessions and/or feel free to leave comments/questions on the Community with our tag ‘SAP Business Network for Suppliers’.

More to come as the rollout progresses!

entry.title: Re: Super Admin Access
entry.content:
Hi @ahounsome ,

Here comes some explanation for your consideration on the administrator management. 

A super administrator is a privileged user responsible for setting up the instance or inviting others to do so. The first user created after instance provisioning is automatically granted super administrator rights. 

Based on this definition, super administrators will be granted permissions accordingly. We adhere to a minimal privilege policy while ensuring a smooth customer onboarding experience, reducing manual steps and accommodating users with limited SuccessFactors knowledge.

  • access to your SF system
  • access Manage Provisioning Access page to invite and send approval note to Partner for their access to Provisioning system
  • access Manage RBP Admin Access page to grant himself or others to setup RBP.  (User search is a pre-q for adding new admin)
  • access Manage Super Admin Access page to grant others to take the administration work. (User search is a pre-q for adding new admin)
  • access Learning Admin console to setup Learning if Learning module is enabled.

The Admin Overseer, formerly the RBP Super Admin, is a restricted admin role dedicated to admin user management, with no operational responsibilities, and access to Manage RBP Admin Access and Manage Super Admin Access only after instance is setup.

Regards,

Fannie Guan




Should background technical users be set-up as Super Admins? Should our IT administrators be defined as Super Admins rather than Admin Overseers? What is the rationale for including Provisioning and Learning Administration access as part of Super Admin access, given that additional permissions are still required to manage most functional areas of the system?

entry.title: SAP Runs SAP at Sapphire 2026: How SAP Runs Its Own Enterprise
entry.content:
At Sapphire 2026, SAP runs SAP focuses on a straightforward question:
what can be learned from how SAP operates its own business using its own technology?

Across Orlando and Madrid, the SAP runs SAP track brings together more than 50 sessions with over 16 hours of content, supported by SAP’s internal IT experts who are directly involved in running these systems. The intent is not to present a vision, but to provide visibility into how agents, applications, and data are applied in a real enterprise environment.

We offer dedicated sessions tailored to specific buying centers—including COO, CIO, CFO, CHRO, and CRO audiences. These sessions reflect the real challenges SAP is facing and demonstrate how we are using our own technology and solutions to achieve better outcomes. Across finance, HR, procurement, CX, and operations, the emphasis is consistently on how things work in practice: how decisions are supported by data, how workflows are structured, and how AI is embedded into day-to-day operations.

What differentiates the experience is how these sessions extend beyond the stage into direct interaction at the SAP runs SAP Experience Hub.

The Expert Bar provides a direct way to continue the conversation. After a session, or independently, participants can speak with SAP experts, ask follow-up questions, and discuss how specific topics translate into their own context.

The C-suite Experience offers a more structured perspective. It allows visitors to explore how SAP approaches transformation from different executive viewpoints, such as finance, HR, or operations, linking individual decisions to broader business outcomes.

The SAP runs SAP Theater complements this with Ask the Expert sessions that revisit key topics in a more focused setting.

Together, these elements are designed to make it easier to move between listening, discussing, and exploring. Instead of a single entry point, the experience allows visitors to engage with the material at different levels, depending on their interests and priorities.

SAP runs SAP does not focus on what could be done, but on what is already in place and what can be learned from it. For leaders looking for real-world proof, measurable outcomes, and practical lessons, it offers a more concrete view of how AI and enterprise systems come together in practice, and how these insights can be applied in their own organizations.

SAP Sapphire 2026 offers a chance to explore this firsthand—and to engage directly with the people and experiences behind it. To explore SAP runs SAP sessions in more detail, please refer to the session catalogs:

Orlando and Madrid

Get a first exciting taste of what’s ahead—watch our short YouTube teaser videos and get inspired by the insights and perspectives from our SAP runs SAP speakers.

Sapphire is one moment in time, but the discussion continues beyond the event.

The Inside SAP IT YouTube Live series offers a more in-depth look at how SAP operates its IT landscape, with a focus on concrete decisions and implementation topics.

Join the upcoming session here:

entry.title: Re: How has RISE with SAP changed ownership across teams...
entry.content: Yeah, that’s a good way to put it. With RISE with SAP it really feels like Basis doesn’t disappear, it just shifts focus. Instead of owning the full stack, the role becomes more about coordination, understanding what SAP controls vs what the business still owns, and making sure nothing falls through the cracks. That “bridge” you mention is probably the biggest change. I also agree it’s not necessarily simpler, just a different model that forces teams to think more in terms of orchestration than execution.

entry.title: Re: Demo Scenario: Clean Core Extensibility with ABAP Cloud and SAP Build
entry.content:
@Jelena_Perfiljeva Thanks for the detailed feedback

1. The clean core extensibility whitepaper mentioned in the pre-read covers this in page 7. Or do you mean the arguments presented there are not convincing on business value? Would love to understand your take on clean core extensibility with reasoning.

2. Do consider the cost of upgrade projects with delays/budget overshots, bottlenecks to adopt new technology/innovations among others in the "before" solution

3. Yes, you are right on the decision needing more information. This is intended as a demo/sample only. Consider the application extension methodology for actual decision making

4. Sure, cost of services being weighed against the potential benefit in the specific customer context is fair. Scale is one aspect, there could be more. Nevertheless, the intent is to show what is possible. 

5. Thanks for sharing. As stated under assumptions, we here assume no fit-to-standard solution exists (although this may or may not be factually correct)

6. Thanks for the idea. However, there are different categories and segments of readers of such blog posts. On one hand, there are experts like yourselves who've been around and worked on stuff for 20 years, keeping track of the changing SAP landscape. On the other hand, there are others who are trying to grasp the idea of clean core extensibility and how this is different to the classic way of working. Yes, SAP has been talking about clean core for a while now but such high-level examples serve the audience that still don't understand the big picture. 

In general, I like your idea of having a more detailed version talking about architectural/cost trade-offs with a real customer example but unless we can name the customer to gain credibility for such a scenario, there would always be more factors one could bring up for consideration.

entry.title: Joule Skill Call Action: auto-generated branch conditions crash
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I want to report a bug in Joule Studio's Skill Builder that occurs recently when adding a Call Action step to a Joule Skill.

**Environment**
- SAP Joule Studio (Skill Builder)

**Steps to Reproduce**
1. Create an Action Project 
2. Release and publish the action
3. In Joule Studio, create a Joule Skill and add a Call Action step referencing the above action
4. Observe the auto-generated branches on the canvas

**What Happens**
When the Call Action step is added, two branches are automatically generated: Success and Default error. Both branches contain an auto-generated condition expression that references `body.output._statusCode`. 

Since the action has no statusCode output field,  the expression fails immediately with:

`EL1008E: Property or field 'default' cannot be found on object of type 'Map' - maybe not public or not valid?` 

Set variables action executed with errors due to invalid scripting expression

**Impact**
- The skill crashes on BOTH the Success and Default Error branches
- The branch conditions are not editable in the canvas, so there is no way to manually fix the expression
- The skill is completely unusable

**Expected Behavior**
Branches should either:
- Not be auto-generated, OR
- Use a safe default expression 

Is this a a new update? Is there a fix or official workaround planned?

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Powering SAP Cloud Transformation with Advanced Data Migration and Continuous Testing
entry.content:
@RACHID_AITBOUALE 

In addition, the following was also recommended.

The recommendation for this type of integration is to avoid letting the AI agent call SAP directly or without control.... It should go through a controlled layer such as SAP BTP, SAP Integration Suite, API Management, or another governed middleware layer.

 From a testing point of view, the key recommendation is to test the API contracts with Tosca API Testing. This includes checking schemas, required fields, permissions, error handling, idempotency, logs, usage limits, and general API behavior.

entry.title: Adding a Decomposition Tree to SAP Analytics Cloud with D3.js
entry.content:
A practical walk-through of building a Power BI-style decomposition tree as an SAP Analytics Cloud custom widget. We will take it from a blank canvas to a smooth, data-bound, production-feeling visualization. This post is written for SAC analytics developers and anyone who has stared at the custom widget SDK wondering exactly where to start.

Quick side note before we jump in: I used Claude AI to help write the code, which made shipping this significantly faster and saved me from a lot of trial and error!

The Gap in the Market

SAP Analytics Cloud has a highly capable chart catalog. However, if you have ever used Power BI's decomposition tree, you know the one major feature it lacks is a left-to-right hierarchical drill-down. You know the exact interaction: each node has a value bar, you click to expand the next level, and you can instantly answer the question of where a specific number is coming from.

This is easily one of the most loved analytics interactions in the BI space. It is exactly how finance, operations, and revenue teams investigate variance across the board: Total → Region → Country → Customer → Product. In SAC, you can build a tree table or a sunburst, but neither of those options feels quite like that intuitive drill-down.

So, I decided to build one. This post covers the story of how it works, what surprised me along the way, and how you can use the final result.

What We Are Building

The goal was to create a custom widget that delivers on all fronts:

  • Appears in the SAC widget picker just like any built-in chart.

  • Lets designers drag dimensions and a measure into a Builder feed (the standard SAC data-binding user experience).

  • Renders a left-to-right tree starting with a synthetic Total root, followed by one column per dimension level. Each node needs a label, a value, and a horizontal bar sized by the measure.

  • Animates expand and collapse actions smoothly.

  • Survives high-cardinality dimensions (like 10,000 unique customers) without freezing the user's browser.

  • Plays nicely with linked analysis filters originating from other widgets.

                          ┌──────────────┐
                          │  Region      │
                          │  Europe      │
                          │  ███████░░   │
                          └──────────────┘
                          ┌──────────────┐
┌──────────────┐    ┌──→  │  Region      │
│  Total       │    │     │  Americas    │
│  Sales       │ ───┤     │  █████░░░░   │
│  ████████    │    │     └──────────────┘
└──────────────┘    │     ┌──────────────┐
                    └──→  │  Region      │
                          │  Asia        │
                          │  ███░░░░░░   │
                          └──────────────┘

Click Americas and it reveals USA, Canada, and Mexico as a third column. Click any of those and it reveals their respective customers.

A Custom Widget Primer

If you have never built one before, SAC custom widgets are essentially Web Components loaded by SAC directly into your story. Each widget consists of a JSON manifest plus one or more JavaScript files and an icon. The JSON declares the widget's name, properties, methods, events, and optionally a data binding feed configuration. The JavaScript files register custom HTML elements (com-yourname-widget) and implement the lifecycle hooks that SAC calls natively (onCustomWidgetBeforeUpdate, onCustomWidgetAfterUpdate, etc.).

You upload the JSON to SAC, then upload a ZIP file containing the JS files and the icon. SAC hosts these resources and serves them to the browser whenever your widget is placed on a canvas.

Architecture Overview

Three main files handle all of the heavy lifting:

  • decomposition_tree.json: The manifest. This declares properties, methods, events, and the data binding configurations (the dimensions feed and the measure feed).

  • decomposition_tree.js: The main Web Component. This handles the D3 layout, click handling, and transitions.

  • decomposition_tree_styling.js: The Styling panel. This controls the color picker, value scale, Top-N settings, and the default expand depth.

Step 1: The Manifest

The JSON manifest is mostly administrative bookkeeping, but two specific parts are critical.

Web Components

"webcomponents": [
  { "kind": "main",    "tag": "com-example-decomposition-tree",         "url": "/decomposition_tree.js" },
  { "kind": "styling", "tag": "com-example-decomposition-tree-styling", "url": "/decomposition_tree_styling.js" }
]

Using forward-slash prefixed URLs ensures the exact same JSON works whether you do a ZIP upload or choose to self-host.

Data Binding

"dataBindings": {
  "treeData": {
    "feeds": [
      { "id": "dimensions", "type": "dimension",            "description": "Hierarchy levels, outer to inner" },
      { "id": "measure",    "type": "mainStructureMember",  "description": "Bar size and node aggregation" }
    ]
  }
}

This single block does an incredible amount of work for us. It tells SAC to auto-generate the feed-config Builder, wires up the data fetch when designers bind a model, and exposes the final result set to the widget as this.treeData.data and this.treeData.metadata at runtime.

Step 2: From a Flat Result Set to a Tree

When dimensions and a measure are bound, SAC hands the widget a flat array of rows that looks something like this:

[
  { dimensions_0: { id: "...", label: "Americas" },
    dimensions_1: { id: "...", label: "USA" },
    dimensions_2: { id: "...", label: "Acme Corp" },
    measure_0:    { raw: 320000, formatted: "320K", unit: "USD" } },
  // ...
]

To draw a decomposition tree, we must convert this into a hierarchy. The folding process is straightforward. We walk each row, descend through the dimension keys, create child nodes on demand, and accumulate the measure:

const root = { name: "Total", value: 0, children: [], _childMap: {} };

for (const row of rows) {
  const v = Number(row.measure_0?.raw) || 0;
  let cursor = root;
  cursor.value += v;
  for (const dimKey of dimAliases) {
    const label = row[dimKey]?.label || "(empty)";
    let child = cursor._childMap[label];
    if (!child) {
      child = { name: label, value: 0, children: [], _childMap: {} };
      cursor._childMap[label] = child;
      cursor.children.push(child);
    }
    child.value += v;
    cursor = child;
  }
}

That is the entire core algorithm. The rest of the code is just performance and user experience scaffolding built around it.

Step 3: Surviving High Cardinality

The first time I bound a Customer dimension with thousands of distinct values, the browser completely hung. Folding ten thousand rows in memory is fine, but rendering ten thousand SVG groups will destroy performance.

The fix is utilizing a Top N + Others grouping.

At every level of the tree, after sorting the children descending by their measure, keep only the top N values and roll the remainder into a single leaf node labeled "Others (count)".

n.children.sort((a, b) => b.value - a.value);
if (n.children.length > topN) {
  const top  = n.children.slice(0, topN);
  const rest = n.children.slice(topN);
  const othersValue = rest.reduce((s, c) => s + c.value, 0);
  n.children = [...top, {
    name: "Others (" + rest.length + ")",
    value: othersValue,
    _isOthers: true
  }];
}

Step 4: Keeping User State Across Data Refreshes

When a user clicks a bar in a different widget wired up via linked analysis, SAC pushes a new result set to your tree. If you naively recompute the tree, the user's expand state completely vanishes. To fix this, you have to track user actions by path string rather than by node reference.

Every click adds an entry to your tracking sets. After a rebuild, walk the new tree and apply the overrides:

this._root.each(n => {
  const path = pathOf(n);
  if (this._userExpandedPaths.has(path))  this._collapsedNodes.delete(n);
  if (this._userCollapsedPaths.has(path)) this._collapsedNodes.add(n);
});

Step 5: Dimension Chips at Runtime

Story viewers often want to try the tree using different decomposition orders without calling a designer. To solve this, I added a clickable dimension chip bar above the tree. Clicking a chip removes that level from the fold, and clicking a disabled chip adds it right back. The tree naturally re-folds on the fly.

Step 6: Visual Polish

To make this feel like a native, premium product, I added some final touches:

  • A clean system font stack.

  • An 8px corner radius with white nodes on a faint gray background.

  • A 3px blue accent strip on the left edge of nodes that have children, providing a subtle visual affordance that the node can expand.

  • A soft drop-shadow on hover using an SVG filter.

  • A selected-path highlight where clicking a node lights up the specific path from the root directly to that node with blue borders and blue links.

Grab the Code and Try It Out

If you want to dive deeper, check out the full source code, or just grab the files to test this in your own SAP Analytics Cloud tenant, I have put everything up on GitHub : Decomposition Tree 

You can find the full repository here: 

I highly recommend reading through the repository README. It contains the complete configuration table, script API details, and some extra technical notes that did not fit into this post.

How to Upload the Widget to Your SAC Tenant

Getting this running in your own environment only takes a minute or two. You do not need to compile anything. Just follow these steps:

  1. Download the files from the GitHub repository.

  2. Create a ZIP file containing exactly three items: decomposition_tree.js, decomposition_tree_styling.js, and icon.png. Leave the JSON file outside of the ZIP.

  3. Log in to your SAP Analytics Cloud tenant.

  4. Navigate to the Custom Widgets area. You can usually find this by going to the left hand navigation menu, clicking Stories, and selecting the Custom Widgets tab.

  5. Click the + Create icon (or Add button) to upload a new widget.

  6. Upload the manifest first. Select the decomposition_tree.json file from your downloaded folder.

  7. Upload the resources. When SAC prompts you for the resource files, select the ZIP file you created in step two.

Happy decomposing!

entry.title: How do I expose views from Hana via Service Layer v2?
entry.content:
Question has been asked before, but answers do not apply to my scenario and are generally outdated.

As in subject: I need to expose some of my views through Service Layer. The only method I've currently found is to create wrappers in form of Calculation Views: this approach works, but it's incredibly cumbersome. I really need an easier way, that can possibly be implemented in a setup procedure (e.g. via SQL scripts, DI Api, direct Hana access via ADO.NET or a combination of these methods).

I've already tried using the /b1s/v2/SQLQueries endpoint: unfortunately, this endpoint won't allow the creation of complex queries, seriously limiting its potential; several SQL commands are not supported and the query gets discarded. 

Between Google searches and AI chats I get there are other methods, but I honestly can't wrap my head around this issue. To recap, what I need is:
- creation of several views with a one-off script or unattended program (no GUI, no wizards, no packages and so on): creation is trivial, as it's just a CREATE instruction
- exposition of these views via Service Layer
- interrogation of these views via Service Layer

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Business Calc Amount1/2 - SAP SuccessFactors Variable...
entry.content:
Hi @Joana_Rochete , please take the lead in organizing your own troubleshooting now that I've shown that it is working in a demo environment. It is the nature of Variable Pay that configuration is complex and anything you change anywhere may have an impact on everything else. From the get go when I saw your first message and screenshots I wondered why you didn't respect the format for non-decimal numbers which should always be #,##0 (with a 0 at the end). If I were you, since you are seeing a bit of a breakthrough with the first idea, I would test the following things:

  1. Assign a Money format with format #,##0.##### to these two columns AS WELL AS TO ALL OTHER other standard columns you are using in your configuration. Then rerun bonus calculation and relaunch a new worksheet to see the outcome on the two fields you are interested in.
  2. If you are seeing progress with correct values in the Bus Calc Amount 1 and 2, then slowly adjust your configurations to single out where the issue was until everything is working as expected.

Please don't edit the original post but add new comments with new screenshots in case you still need help.

If it can be of any help, I am attaching the XML from my demo environment.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1_1l3mz9YmvTQ4YdvTr7u0rogJZMNIB6K/view?usp=sharing

Xavier

entry.title: Requirements created in SAP Cloud ALM can now be seen in SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Have you ever asked yourself how the requirements created in Fit to Standard workshop can be shown and used in SAP Signavio? This is now possible thanks to the new capability in the integration between SAP Signavio and SAP Cloud ALM, released this April 30th.  

The Challenge

Previously, Process Modelers faced challenges in accessing process requirements directly within SAP Signavio. They were required to navigate to SAP Cloud ALM to identify applicable requirements for their process models and then return to Signavio to implement those requirements during the modeling process. 

How it Works

With the updated workflow, users can now initiate the creation of requirements directly from SAP Signavio, which seamlessly redirects them to the relevant section in SAP Cloud ALM. After creating and linking the requirements to the project, scope, and the synchronized process model in SAP Cloud ALM, these requirements will automatically appear in SAP Signavio, organized by project and scope. This streamlined integration ensures that all necessary information is conveniently accessible within SAP Signavio, enabling users to manage process modeling and requirements efficiently from a single interface. 

The new feature uses the best of both products. You can continue to create requirements in SAP Cloud ALM within your project, and once you take the following steps, those requirements become directly applicable to your SAP Signavio process models: 

  •  Sync the SAP Signavio process model to SAP Cloud ALM, where it becomes part of a solution process 
  • Publish the solution process in SAP Cloud ALM 
  • The solution process is added to your project's scope which has enabled Custom Solution scenarios and 
  • The requirement is assigned to solution process or its elements 

Regardless of whether your requirement is assigned to a solution process, process flow, process activity or value flow, all related requirements will appear directly in the SAP Signavio diagram page for your process model.   The new Requirements section in the Diagram page in the screenshot below shows requirements grouped by project and scope, providing details of the requirement together with navigation button for more details in SAP Cloud ALM, as well as an opportunity to create requirements in SAP Cloud ALM, by clicking the Create button: 

During the Fit-to-Standard workshop, when requirements are gathered in a scenario where prior assignments have already been completed, the requirements can be conveniently accessed directly beneath the process model. This streamlined approach not only saves time but also centralizes the brainstorming process, fostering efficiency and collaboration.

How to Enable it

Don't want to enable requirements for your workspace? No problem, you can easily opt in and opt out. The following screenshot presents the necessary switch which enables the requirements for your workspace: 

After the requirements are enabled, you need to allow their appearance on the Diagram page, by selecting Requirements in the following page:

Final Thoughts

Regardless if you are Process modeler or Requirements creator in Fit to Standard workshop, the Diagram page now provides you detailed view of requirements related to your process model. Implementing the whole workflow from creating through assignment of requirements and their appearance in SAP Signavio is easy with a step-by-step guide which you can find in our documentation:  

https://help.sap.com/docs/signavio-process-manager/user-guide/viewing-requirements-in-sap-signavio

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content:
Hi @Chris1973 ,

First of all, thank you for taking the time to go through my messages and for sharing such detailed suggestions—I really appreciate it.

At the moment, we are following the same approach you described: creating/updating customers via API and then assigning the Collection Profile using the Schedule Collections Management Jobs – Mass Changes functionality.

The challenge we’re facing is that these mass change jobs cannot be scheduled dynamically on a daily basis. For example, when we create a job like “Assignment of Collection Profiles to Business Partners” in the Application Jobs app and try to use a parameter such as “Created On,” there is no option to dynamically populate this field based on the job execution date.

Typically, SAP provides a “Dynamic Dates” option for such parameters, but in this case, it’s not available. This makes it difficult to schedule a single recurring job for daily execution.

As a workaround, we are currently creating individual jobs for each day. For instance, for a full month, we create 31 separate jobs, each with the “Created On” date hardcoded to match the respective execution date. Ideally, we would prefer to schedule a single job that runs daily, with the “Created On” field automatically set to “Today” instead of a fixed date.

Please let me know if there’s an alternative approach or workaround we could explore here.

entry.title: Re: Sapces in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @SJSAPIDL_2,

​1. 
​Yes, we have same object in two spaces but when it shows in 2nd space as a shared but with a distinction between the "Source" and the "Target." An object (Table or View) is physically owned by one space (the Source Space), but it can be shared with and utilized by multiple other spaces.


​2. If we share Table-A from Space-1 to Space-2, where does it exist?
​When you share Table-A from Space-1 (Source) to Space-2 (Target), the object remains in both spaces, but they play different roles

When you share an object, you are not creating a second copy of the data. Space-2 is essentially looking at a "pointer" or a virtual reference to the data sitting in Space-1. This ensures a "Single Source of Truth."

Thank You.

entry.title: Re: WSAEWOULDBLOCK: Resource temporarily Unavailable : Wh...
entry.content:
This is an old post but it comes up as a top result on Google search for this error. Which is still happening in 2026. 🙂

The note mentioned by the legendary Matt Fraser has been removed. I found this new note: 3301728 that explains the error and has a link to another note. If this note gets obsoleted too, just go to the SAP Support portal (whatever its current name is, SAP for Me right now) and just search for WSAEWOULDBLOCK.

If the issue is consistent and not sporadic and your connection details are correct, 99% it's the firewall issue. Contact your network team. Hope this helps someone.

🚀A agenda oficial do SAP Inside Track Recife 2026 está no ar!

No dia 23 de maio, a comunidade SAP se reúne em Recife para uma edição muito especial. Em 2026, celebramos 10 anos da SAP Community Recife, uma trajetória construída com colaboração, troca de conhecimento, networking e muita dedicação de profissionais que acreditam no poder da comunidade.

A programação deste ano foi pensada para conectar temas atuais do ecossistema SAP com experiências reais de mercado, passando por SAP S/4HANA, SAP BTP, Business AI, Clean Core, SAP Signavio, Fiori, manutenção digital, jornada Cloud e tendências para o futuro do consultor SAP.

Ao longo do dia, teremos palestras, momentos de networking, coffee breaks, almoço, sorteios e encerramento oficial.

entry.title: Lessons Learned from Hypercare to AMS Transition in S/4HANA Projects
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

In several SAP S/4HANA projects, I have observed that the transition from hypercare to AMS support is often more challenging than anticipated, even when KT activities, documentation, and sign-offs are completed successfully.

Systems may appear stable during the early go-live period, but operational issues can surface later during peak business cycles such as month-end processing, interface spikes, or batch job overlaps.

Some areas that I personally found important during AMS transition include:

  • Understanding real production behaviour during peak load
  • Identifying critical monitoring periods
  • Capturing lessons learned from hypercare
  • Enhancing coordination between project and AMS teams
  • Building AMS team troubleshooting confidence before full takeover

In certain situations, temporary workarounds or manual monitoring activities performed during hypercare were not fully documented during KT, which later created operational ambiguity for support teams.

I would like to hear from the community:

  • What are the key challenges you have seen during Hypercare to AMS transition in S/4HANA projects?
  • Are there any best practices your teams follow to improve operational readiness and long-term support stability?

I look forward to hearing different experiences and approaches from the community.

Best regards,
Raghu Garapati

entry.title: Re: Where to create new FS item : Dev with transport to Q...
entry.content: Hi Martin, You are right that in past times we usually did not transport FS items in FSV. Especially, after incorporating Group Reporting, SAP has made it more possible to transport Financial Statement (FS) items in SAP, particularly for S/4HANA Group Reporting. This involves using the Customizing Transport Request mechanism to move master data and configuration from development to quality/production. Nowadays, Group Reporting is widely being included in the mainstream of SAP. Any changes carried out in S/4HANA, to Define FS Items Fiori app (F3297) should automatically prompt for a transport request if the system is configured for Automatic Recording of Changes. Kindly ensure that attributes such as FS Item Type (BS vs P&L), elimination rules, and breakdown categories are correctly captured in the transport. Regards, Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to split PPV when posting a Goods Rece...
entry.content:
Hi Goody-1

Yes, there are several ways to split Purchase Price Variance (PPV) when posting a goods receipt (GR) in SAP, depending on whether you want to split the accounting entries, the material valuation, or the physically received quantity.

Finance Material Valuation:
In SAP S/4HANA, you can configure the system to automatically split price differences into different General Ledger (GL) accounts based on variance categories. This provides more granular visibility in your Profit and Loss (P&L) statement.

1. Usually PRD account is used to reverse the initial posting in SAP. So, instead of all variances hitting a single PRD account, if separate GL accounts are created for each variance category then PRD values can be split.

2. Define a Price Difference Split Profile in the SPRO path:
IMG Financial Accounting > General Ledger Accounting > Periodic Processing > Integration > Materials Management
3. Assign the splitting profile to your company code

Inventory Split can also be achieved using the Split Valuation in SAP-MM

Regards,

Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Re: Unable to Assign Collection Management Attributes via...
entry.content: Hi Rohit - When the "Inbound service not found" error for SAP UDM Collections Management indicates that the OData service (UDMO_COLLECTION_WORKLIST) is not activated in transaction /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE or that a service group binding is missing, causing a 404/MED033 error. Please Consult your technical team. Regards, PS

entry.title: Errors during Read Jobs execution in Identity Provisioning - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition activation
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently in the process of activating a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition tenant. As part of the initial setup, we accessed the administrator console, created an additional user beyond the default admin user, and proceeded to execute the Read Jobs for the different source systems configured in the Identity Provisioning Service (IPS).

During the execution of the Read Jobs for the systems S4-my438910-source and IAS formy438924 - source, we encountered the following errors:

Error type 1 — User provisioning failures:

 
 
user:56309a19-64ec-45b8-92d3-1ef6fe509112,system=S4HANAmy438910 - target,time=2026-05-05T18:47:19.646+00,error=Person SALES.ADOPTION@STRUCTUM-CO.COM does not exist.,content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities
 
 
user:63a9dc7c-f700-4521-ba3b-d6e44de2ea2e,system=S4HANAmy438910 - target,time=2026-05-05T18:47:19.816+00,error=Person OSCAR.QUIJANO@STRUCTUM-CO.COM does not exist.,content=<hidden content> - Set property ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true to display the content of failed entities

Error type 2 — Group provisioning failures (7 similar errors):

entry.title: Re: Project Systems How to Change a WBS element after it...
entry.content:
Hi Margie,
Chris has explained the process well. An alternate solution would be to:
Sometimes, we have seen things getting stuck in SAP because the response from the user was unacceptable. Since you are unable to do anything with WBS ID correction, you may try these steps:

1. Ensure that CJI3 is pulling information as Project Actual Cost Line Items, and you are unable to reverse the posting as explained by Chris.
2. KB11n - Manual Reposting of Costs to transfer primary costs from one cost object to another without reversing the original financial document.

  • Use Scr Variant - 'WBS element/order' and input type 'List Entry'
  • Put WBS element (old) with cost element and Put WBS element (new) with quantity if applicable

3. Now you can run CJ88 - Settlement of WBS

4. Note: Previous Year Issues: If the settlement occurred in a previous fiscal year, reversal is more complex and may affect depreciation.

Regards, Pranay Sahay

entry.title: Extensibility of Manage Prices - Sales (F4111) : Change Standard Field Logic
entry.content:
Hello Folks,

As my current Project Requirement, they are asking to change Standard fields logic in Manage Prices - Sales (F4111) application.

By Default, "Price Group" is coming with pre-populated values. They are asking to make this field as BLANK at initial load and replace "Value Help"  (F4) values. 

1) Make "Price Group" as Blank

2) Replace Value Help of this field with Complete new Custom Value Help

Please check attached screenshot for your reference.

Can we change standard app existing fields logic? IS THIS POSSIBLE AT All?

As per my knowledge and findings, via UI ADAPTATION, we can only add NEW CUSTOM Fields in this application and make our custom code on newly added Custom field only.

entry.title: Re: When One Release Has to Reach Many SAP Cloud Integration Tenants
entry.content:
Hi @dougliu 

I’ve worked with both approaches depending on the client maturity and landscape setup.

For some clients, we use SAP Cloud Transport Management (TMS) to move artifacts across DEV/QA/PRD tenants in a more controlled and governed way. But I’ve also worked with newer SAP IS clients where transports are still handled manually by downloading the iFlow/package ZIPs and importing them into the target tenant.

Honestly, I still see a lot of customers operating in a semi-manual way, especially smaller teams or environments that were initially set up quickly and later scaled.

I personally haven’t worked with Content Agent Service (CAS) yet, so I can’t speak much on real-world adoption there from my side yet.

entry.title: Серія вебкастів SAP у травні
entry.content:
Травень — насичений місяць для SAP-спільноти! Найбільша подія року та експертні вебкасти з безпеки, оновлень та інтеграції — ми зібрали всі ключові можливості для навчання в одному місці.

Обирайте найцікавіші для вас сесії та реєструйтесь за лінками нижче.

Час вказано за Києвом | Мова вебінарів — англійська.

AI, інновації та майбутнє SAP

  • 12 - 13 травня
    SAP Sapphire Virtual
    12 травня: початок о 15:30, остання сесія завершується о 23:45
    13 травня: початок о 16:00, остання сесія завершується о 23:30

    Найбільша подія SAP року — тепер у віртуальному форматі, наживо. Приєднуйтесь, щоб побачити сміливе бачення SAP щодо того, як бізнес працюватиме в майбутньому.
    Головна тема цього року: The Beginning of Better — переосмислення того, як працює бізнес в епоху AI. У центрі уваги — оновлений Joule: новий центральний робочий простір, який проактивно покращує бізнес-операції та об'єднує людей і AI для досягнення реальних результатів.
    З вітальним кейнотом виступлять CEO Christian Klein та топ-керівники SAP: Muhammad Alam - член виконавчого комітету, Sebastian Steinhaeuser – COO, Philipp Herzig - CTO. Вони розкажуть, як SAP забезпечує потужну основу з застосунків та даних, яка дозволяє AI досягати реальних бізнес-результатів.
    🎾 Спеціальний гість — Roger Federer 13 травня о 22:30 - 23:30 поділиться інсайтами про стійкість та стратегічне мислення для лідерів бізнесу у будь-якій галузі.

    Що на вас чекає: живі демонстрації оновленого Joule та AI-агентів, live "Ask the Expert" Q&A сесії з експертами SAP, тематичні треки: AI, Cloud ERP, Joule, Data & Analytics, Supply Chain, Build & Integrate.
    🎓 Бонус для учасників: 25% знижка на SAP Certification та безкоштовний доступ до SAP Learning Hub, event edition.
    Детальніше про спікерів та повний каталог сесій — за посиланням. Використовуйте фільтри та пошук, щоб знайти найцікавіші для вас сесії, додавайте їх до персонального розкладу та відстежуйте в особистому кабінеті. Всі прямі трансляції будуть доступні у записі після події.
    Додавайте до календаря — SAP Sapphire 2026 це не просто подія, це точка відліку нової ери бізнесу з AI. 🚀

Безпека SAP

  • 21 травня, 11:30 – 12:30
    SAP Security Webcast in May: Quantum Safe Cryptography – Surviving the Quantum Apocalypse - EMEA
    Щомісяця SAP обирає найактуальніші теми з безпеки для клієнтів. У травні — квантова криптографія та її вплив на ваше SAP-середовище.
    Квантові комп'ютери вже найближчим часом можуть зламати основу сучасної криптографії. Дізнайтесь, що це означає для SAP бізнес-застосунків, які варіанти захисту існують, та як спланувати post-quantum cryptography міграцію. В результаті — готовий план дій для захисту SAP-ландшафту в епоху квантових технологій.
    Записи попередніх вебкастів доступні за посиланням.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • 21 травня, 16:15 – 17:00
    In-Depth with SAP Cloud ERP May Edition – What's New in SAP Central Business Configuration
    Огляд останніх оновлень та нових можливостей SAP Central Business Configuration. У програмі: огляд нових функцій, живі демонстрації та сесія Q&A. Sales, project managers, presales, consultants та business users можуть ознайомитися з найновішими можливостями SAP CBC.

Інтеграція

  • 26 травня, 17:00 – 18:00
    SAP Integration Suite - Monthly Update May
    SAP Integration Suite — це iPaaS платформа для проектування, розробки, виконання та управління інтеграційними потоками, API та бізнес-подіями, що з'єднують хмарні та локальні процеси, застосунки і дані.
    У програмі: Product Updates та інтерактивна сесія з експертом — Integration Black Belt або #AskAnythingIntegration, де панель експертів відповість на ваші запитання.

Травень — це місяць, коли SAP-спільнота збирається разом: навчається, ділиться досвідом та готується до майбутнього. Не пропустіть жодної можливості — реєструйтесь, додавайте до календаря та приєднуйтесь! 💡

entry.title: Re: How to Backup SAP commerce cloud promotion from One e...

In Commerce Clould OOTB feature, there is no functionality available to backup or restore only promotions directly.  However, you can achieve this by utilizing the impex export and import features to extract and import the promotion data.

3422066 - How to Extract Details of the Promotions created in SAP Commerce Cloud

Extracting Details of Created Promotions

entry.title: Important Update: What's Changing in US Federal Regulatory Tax Reporting and How SAP has you covered
entry.content:
OVERVIEW
What SAP Users Need to Know About the Tax Year 2026/Reporting Year 2027 Transition for IRS Reporting.

As we move into 2026, the IRS is implementing one of the most significant updates to its information returns filing infrastructure in decades. The legacy Filing Information Returns Electronically (FIRE) system, which many of you currently use for submitting 1099 forms and other informational reports, will retire on December 31, 2026.

Starting with Tax Year 2026 filings (due in early 2027), form submissions must be made through the Information Returns Intake System (IRIS). This change directly impacts how SAP customers generate, validate, and file forms such as 1099-MISC, 1099-INT, 1099-NEC, 1099-K, 1099-G, 1099-C, 1042-S, 1099-R, and 1098-T using SAP Document and Reporting Compliance in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition/Private Edition/On-premise. SAP ERP (ECC) is supported by ABAP Program (RFIDYYWT).

We are here to help you navigate this transition smoothly. Below, we outline the key changes, benefits, and actionable steps to ensure continued compliance with minimal disruption.

JOIN OUR UPCOMING WEBINARS

SAP has planned two webinars for customers and partners which will provide an overview and awareness about the IRIS Transition. Please plan to attend one of these:

• June 2, 2026, 11 AM PST on SAP ON24 - Registration link here
• June 9, 2026, 11 AM PST at ASUG - Registration link here

WHY IRS IS MOVING FROM FIRE TO IRIS

The FIRE system, built on decades-old technology, is being replaced by IRIS — a modern, web-based platform designed to improve accuracy, security, and efficiency.

Key Differences:

AspectFIRE(Legacy)IRIS(New)
File FormatsFlat text files (Publication 1220 format)Structured XML for automated e-filing (A2A) or CSV for portal uploads
ValidationPost-submission rejectionsReal-time error checking and instant corrections
TCC RequirementExisting FIRE TCCNew IRIS-specific TCC required (starting with 'D')
Filing OptionsLimited automationIRIS Taxpayer Portal (manual/CSV uploads: up to 250 records per file, unlimited files) or XML A2A for high-volume automated filing

IMPORTANT: You'll need a separate IRIS-specific Transmitter Control Code (TCC) starting with 'D'. Existing FIRE TCCs won't work — apply early, as approval can take up to 45 days.

FIRE and IRIS will run in parallel during 2026 (for Tax Year 2025 filings due this year), giving you time to test and prepare. However, after December 31, 2026, FIRE will no longer accept submissions and reports must be filed via IRIS.

HOW THIS AFFECTS SAP CUSTOMERS

SAP continues to enhance SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) to align with evolving U.S. statutory requirements, including 1099 and 1042-S reporting. SAP-ERP Reports will also be enhanced to comply with IRIS regulations.

Key Change: No More Copy-A

A key change with the IRIS transition: Copy-A (the IRS-facing red-ink or pre-printable version for paper submission) is no longer necessary and will not be supported.

Two Primary Electronic Filing Methods

SAP Document and Reporting Compliance will provide:

1. CSV file generation — Optimized for direct upload to the IRIS Taxpayer Portal (ideal for manual filing scenarios)
Note: This option is supported only for Tax Year 2026/Filing Year 2027 to facilitate a smooth transition. It will be deprecated in Tax Year 2027 onwards.

2. Direct electronic filing via XML — For secure, automated submission through IRIS Application-to-Application (A2A) communication

Recipient copies (Copy-B PDFs) will continue to be fully supported for distribution to payees/recipients, ensuring you meet furnishing requirements without interruption.

Release Timeline

To support a smooth rollout, SAP plans to release updated DRC functionality for all reports by early October 2026. This will give you sufficient time to install, test, and be ready by early 2027.

This approach gives customers ample time to run thorough testing with the IRIS Assurance Testing System (ATS) during the parallel operation period in 2026 — well before the mandatory shift for Tax Year 2026 filings in 2027.

OUTPUT SUPPORT SUMMARY BY SAP SOLUTION

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition/Private Edition/On-Premise

Legal outputs to be filed with IRIS:

✓ Download of CSV file (Tax Year 2026/Filing Year 2027 only, This output will be deprecated beyond tax year 2026.
✓ E-filing via XML (requires DRC Cloud Edition license - contact your account executives for pricing)
✗ Copy-A (not supported - no longer necessary)

Business partner correspondence:
✓ Copy-B (fully supported)

SAP ERP (ECC)

Legal outputs to be filed with IRIS:
✓ Download of CSV file
✗ E-filing (not supported due to platform limitations)
✗ Copy-A (not supported - no longer necessary)

Business partner correspondence:
✓ Copy-B (fully supported)

BENEFITS YOU'LL SEE WITH IRIS VIA SAP DRC

• Fewer rejections and faster processing thanks to real-time validation
• Larger file capacities and automated bulk filing for high-volume submitters
• Better data accuracy with structured XML/CSV formats, reducing manual rework
• Long-term alignment with IRS modernization — positioning your organization for future form additions and compliance changes

IMMEDIATE NEXT STEPS FOR YOUR TEAM

1. Apply for an IRIS TCC Now

Visit the IRS IRIS portal and submit your application (irs.gov → search "IRIS Application for TCC").

Required Reading:
• IRS Publications 5717, 5718, and 5719
• IRIS 101 and tutorial PDF (provides step-by-step instructions)

Do this ASAP to avoid delays in early 2027!

2. Review Your SAP DRC License and Configuration

Review your current 1099/1042-S/1099-R/1098-T report setups in DRC. Note that new configurations will be required for IRIS compliance.

3. Test Early

Use the time in 2026 to:
• Generate sample IRIS outputs (XML)
• Submit test filings through the IRIS Assurance Testing System (ATS)

Note: IRS will allow you to submit in production only after certification in their ATS system. IRS has not provided an ATS environment for CSV uploads.

4. Stay Updated

Monitor SAP Notes, SAP Community posts on DRC and U.S. withholding.

IRS RESOURCES

• IRIS Portal: irs.gov (search 'IRIS')
• TCC Application: 'IRIS Application for TCC'
- You will need Issuer & Transmitter roles
- TCC Application Tutorial
- After TCC approval, apply for API Client ID for XML (A2A) transmissions
- Complete A2A consent with IRS for e-filing

Key Publications:
• 5717: Taxpayer Portal User Guide
• 5718: Electronic Filing Application to Application (A2A) Specifications
• 5719: Test Package for Information Returns
• Follow "Submit ATS transmissions" to move from Test to Prod
• Instruction 1099-R and 5498

JOIN OUR COMMUNITY WORKSPACES

Stay connected and get the latest updates:

1. Open the Workspace link: USA  — SAP Document and Reporting Compliance
2. USA – SAP Human Capital Management Localization
3. Click the "Request to Join" button
4. Use your work email to submit the request
5. Include your company name, your full name, and your role in the comment section

Subscribe to Regulatory Change Manager for US to follow the releases.

FINAL THOUGHTS

The shift to IRIS is an opportunity to modernize your reporting processes — and with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance's ongoing enhancements and phased rollout, you will be well-equipped to stay compliant effortlessly.

If you have questions about how this affects your specific SAP setup or need help with preparing, please contact our support team.

Thank you for your continued partnership. We're here to support you through this important transition.

Product Management, SAP Globalization
